content: {"analyses": [{"source_url": "https://www.resources.org/resources-radio/which-climate-path-are-we-zeke-hausfather/", "source_title": "Which Climate Path Are We On?, with Zeke Hausfather", "source_type": "web", "source_quality": 0.7, "overall_relevance": 0.65, "year": 0, "authors": [], "venue": "", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "I think the most formative thing for me was probably as a freshman in college, back in 2001, I read Bill McKibben's The End of Nature, which...", "direct_quote": "I think the most formative thing for me was probably as a freshman in college, back in 2001, I read Bill McKibben's The End of Nature, which was the first time I had really become aware of the climate issue and what it meant, and so that book was rea", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}, {"statement": "I spent a while as a college student being a climate activist, ended up later on going to work in the clean tech world as a data scientist, ...", "direct_quote": "I spent a while as a college student being a climate activist, ended up later on going to work in the clean tech world as a data scientist, co-founded a company called Efficiency 2.0 that competed with Opower in the pay-for-based efficiency space.", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}, {"statement": "Zeke Hausfather: No, energy efficiency is hugely important, but 10 years was a good amount of time to spend on it.", "direct_quote": "Zeke Hausfather: No, energy efficiency is hugely important, but 10 years was a good amount of time to spend on it.", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}], "evidence_summary": "Auto-extracted 3 facts."}]}
